title: Performance Marketing-Manager (m/w/d)

company_info.size_raw: mittelständisches Unternehmen im Maschinen- und Anlagenbau

matched_skills: B2B GTM strategy and product positioning
missing_skills: Google Analytics (not listed in CV tools)
Mailchimp or equivalent email marketing tool
core_responsibilities: Develop and execute digital B2B marketing and sales concepts
Own and maintain company website (SEO, SEA, performance analysis)
Create and manage content for website, social media, and newsletters
Coordinate marketing materials (digital/print) with external agencies
Plan and control PR/marketing activities and budget
recommended_emphasis: Highlight B2B marketing experience in industrial/international context to align with machinery and plant engineering focus
Emphasize any KPI reporting or performance tracking work as a proxy for analytics experience
Showcase collateral and content development work (brochures, datasheets) to address print/digital materials requirement
Mention any website or digital touchpoint ownership, even indirectly
Stress German language proficiency and willingness to develop further (B1+ may be borderline)
risk_flags: German proficiency B1+ falls short of 'fließend' (fluent) requirement — high risk of early screening rejection
Core martech stack (Google Analytics, Google Ads, WordPress, Mailchimp, Canva) entirely absent from CV — likely disqualifying for a hands-on Performance Marketing role
Role is operational/executional (SEO, SEA, content creation) whereas candidate profile is strategic (GTM, PMM) — significant scope mismatch
Location (Hemdingen bei Hamburg) is a rural area — likely requires on-site presence, adding a commute/relocation risk from Lüneburg
summary: The candidate has strong B2B marketing strategy credentials and international experience, but lacks the hands-on digital marketing tools (Google Analytics, Google Ads, WordPress, Mailchimp, Canva) that are explicitly required for this performance marketing role. Additionally, the German language requirement ('fließend') exceeds the candidate's stated B1+ level, posing a material screening risk. The role is execution-focused rather than strategy-focused, representing a meaningful scope mismatch.
